verdict_note: SI pre-ACL blocking PASS (SI-001..004, SI-006); SI event -> Splunk forwarding is a documented known gap (D13) recorded as partial, not a hard FAIL.

FMCv.role: Mgmt Center — owns SDA-ACP + Security Intelligence policy

host-splunk.role: listed 'threat' stand-in (otherwise ACL-allowed, reachable) + Splunk SIEM

ise35_control.role: control dst — same allow rule, never listed

cases.objective: Baseline (no SI block): listed dst + control dst reachable; blocklist = Global Block List only

evidence.note: host-splunk reachable at baseline proves it is ACL-allowed, so a later block must be pre-ACL

cases.objective: SI REST API limits are as documented (custom SI lists GUI-only)

evidence.POST_sinetworklists: HTTP 405 (method not allowed) -> custom SI list/feed objects are GUI-only
evidence.global_block_list: SINetworkList c76556bc-6167-11e1-88e8-479de99bfdf1, metadata.readOnly.state=true (cannot add literals via REST)
evidence.networkgroup: SI blocklist rejects NetworkGroup ('is not allowed in Security Intelligence Policy') -> must be a Host/Network object

cases.objective: Apply: add the host-splunk Host object to networks.blocklist via PUT + deploy

evidence.put: GET SI policy -> stripped links/metadata -> appended {network: host-splunk, type Host} -> PUT 200

cases.objective: Verify pre-ACL block: listed dst blocked, control dst still reachable

evidence.conclusion: only the listed dst is dropped; it was ACL-allowed at baseline (SI-001) -> the drop is pre-ACL (Security Intelligence, ahead of the AC rules)

evidence.connection_allow_events: 430002/430003 for control dst 198.18.134.35 and pre-block allows to 198.18.128.51 ARE present (AccessControlRuleAction: Allow) -> connection logging now reaches Splunk (improvement vs C6-era 'no 430xxx')
evidence.si_block_event: ABSENT: post-block blocked pings to 198.18.128.51 produced ZERO Splunk events; 0 events with 'AccessControlRuleAction: Block' / 'Security Intelligence' / SI category / 430001 (intrusion) in the last 45 min
evidence.conclusion: the SI *block* security event for the blocked dst does NOT reach the SIEM (logged to the FMC event viewer only) — the documented D13 gap holds
evidence.classification: known gap / partial, not a hard FAIL of C7

cases.objective: Revert: remove host-splunk from the SI blocklist, restore baseline

evidence.put: GET -> removed host-splunk from networks.blocklist -> PUT 200; read-back blocklist = [Global Block List]

gotchas: POST /object/sinetworklists -> HTTP 405: custom SI list/feed objects are GUI-only over REST.
Global-Block-List is metadata.readOnly.state=true over REST: cannot add literals via API (GUI only).
SI networks.blocklist rejects NetworkGroup ('not allowed in Security Intelligence Policy') -> use a Host or Network object as the 'custom threat list' entry.
fmc_api_call won't take a JSON-object body for the SI PUT -> use curl (token via generatetoken, short TTL, re-auth per call).
Strip links/metadata from the GET body before PUT-ing the SI policy back.
known_gap_D13: FTD security-event syslog (SI/IPS/430001 intrusion) is a separate stream from the LINA %FTD-* syslog delivered to Splunk. Connection events (430002/430003) now land, but the SI block security event does not. Carried as a D-series telemetry follow-up (would light up C6 IPS + C7 SI + connection-block events in Splunk at once).
reversibility: no built config modified; single reversible round-trip (add Host to SI blocklist -> verify -> remove), fully undone and re-verified; lab left at baseline (blocklist = Global Block List only).
